Migration guide for upgrading to MBC CQR: Migration guide for upgrading to MBC CQRS Serverless v1.3.1, including group-based role authorization.
This guide helps you upgrade from v1.3.0: This guide helps you upgrade from v1.3.0 to v1.3.1. Version 1.3.1 has no breaking changes — all existing code continues to work without modification.
There are no breaking changes in v1.3.1. The upgrade is drop-in compatible.
Run the full test suite after upgrading: Run the full test suite after upgrading to confirm no regressions
`RolesGuard` now supports resolving role: `RolesGuard` now supports resolving roles from the user's group memberships stored in the `custom:groups` JWT claim. Groups are resolved using a custom resolver that you implement — the mapping from group IDs to roles is not stored in the JWT.
**This feature is fully opt-in.** Existi: **This feature is fully opt-in.** Existing `@Roles()` checks continue to work without any code changes. You only need to act if you want to use group-derived roles.
Do not add @Injectable() — @GroupRoleRes: Do not add @Injectable() — @GroupRoleResolver() registers the singleton provider
Map group IDs to roles (fetch from DB, c: Map group IDs to roles (fetch from DB, config, or hardcode)
Register the resolver as a provider in y: Register the resolver as a provider in your AppModule:
**Do not add `@Injectable()`** on the re: **Do not add `@Injectable()`** on the resolver class. Adding it alongside `@GroupRoleResolver()` causes a scope conflict (AP027 anti-pattern). `@GroupRoleResolver()` already registers the class as a singleton provider.
**Let failures propagate** — a resolver: **Let failures propagate** — a resolver error surfaces as a 5xx, not a silent 403, making backend outages distinguishable from genuine access denials.
The new AP027 anti-pattern detector (`mb: The new AP027 anti-pattern detector (`mbc_check_anti_patterns`) flags resolver classes that are annotated with both `@GroupRoleResolver()` and `@Injectable()`. If you create a resolver, make sure it has only `@GroupRoleResolver()`.
`getUserContext()` now returns two addit: `getUserContext()` now returns two additional fields on `UserContext`:
Direct roles from the `custom:roles` cla: Direct roles from the `custom:roles` claim for the active tenant
Group IDs from the `custom:groups` claim: Group IDs from the `custom:groups` claim for the active tenant
The existing `tenantRole` (singular stri: The existing `tenantRole` (singular string) is kept for backward compatibility.
If the `custom:groups` JWT claim is malf: If the `custom:groups` JWT claim is malformed or missing, the framework now falls back to an empty group list (no group roles) instead of throwing. This allows the request to proceed with direct roles only.
